title: Live Rebuild Watcher
content_html:
Docs Live Rebuild Watcher

Script:

$HOME/miniconda3/bin/python3 docs-viewer/services/docs_live_rebuild_watcher.py

Purpose

This script watches configured Docs Viewer document and build-media source roots. Markdown changes rebuild same-scope docs payloads and search; supported build-media changes invoke only their configured producer identity.

It is intended as a local development helper for bin/local-studio, not as the primary manual rebuild command.

Scope Detection

The watcher maps each configured document source root directly onto its scope or sub-scope:

  • docs-viewer/source/studio/documents/*.md -> studio
  • docs-viewer/source/analysis/documents/*.md -> analysis
  • docs-viewer/source/library/documents/*.md -> library
  • docs-viewer/source/<scope>/sub-scopes/<sub-scope>/documents/*.md -> <scope>/<sub-scope>
  • <scope source root>/media/mermaid/*.mmd -> that scope's registered mermaid producer and same-basename SVG output

The scope map comes from docs-viewer/config/scopes/docs_scopes.json, which is shared with ./docs-viewer/build/build_docs.py and the Docs Viewer service. The watcher also watches that config file and reconciles its in-memory scope and sub-scope states when the file changes.

It watches configured document roots and registered build-media roots. It does not watch published outputs under:

  • docs-viewer/published/docs/
  • docs-viewer/published/search/
  • site/assets/data/docs/scopes/
  • site/assets/data/search/

Default Command

Run from dotlineform-site/:

$HOME/miniconda3/bin/python3 docs-viewer/services/docs_live_rebuild_watcher.py

Default behavior:

  • polls the source roots every 1.0 second
  • debounces rebuilds for 1.0 second after the most recent source change
  • runs one scope at a time
  • triggers another same-scope pass if more source changes arrive during a rebuild
  • uses targeted docs payload and docs-search updates for source changes at or below the targeted threshold when affected doc ids can be computed from parsed snapshots
  • falls back to a full same-scope docs payload rebuild when affected ids are ambiguous or targeted docs prerequisites are missing
  • falls back to a full same-scope docs-search rebuild when affected ids are ambiguous
  • skips a duplicate same-scope pass when a docs-management write has already rebuilt that source change and left a short-lived watcher suppression marker
  • adds, reloads, or retires scope and sub-scope watch states when docs_scopes.json changes
  • pauses a still-configured scope instead of exiting if its source root is unavailable, then treats files present when that root returns as changes
  • logs computed affected doc ids when targeted search is safe
  • logs the fallback reason when affected ids are unavailable and search must rebuild the full scope
  • renders only the changed Mermaid SVG identity when a configured .mmd source is created or edited, without rebuilding document or search payloads whose token did not change

Optional Flags

  • --repo-root /path/to/dotlineform-site: override root auto-detection by parent-searching for _config.yml
  • --poll-seconds 1.0: polling interval
  • --debounce-seconds 1.0: quiet window before rebuild
  • --targeted-search-threshold 5: maximum changed file count for targeted docs-search updates; use -1 to target whenever the affected ids are safe

What It Runs

When the watcher sees a change for one scope, it runs:

./docs-viewer/build/build_docs.py --scope <scope> --write
./docs-viewer/build/build_search.py --scope <scope> --write --only-doc-ids <ids> --remove-missing

When affected ids are safe, the docs payload command becomes:

./docs-viewer/build/build_docs.py --scope <scope> --write --only-doc-ids <ids>

If targeted docs payload prerequisites are missing, the docs payload step falls back to:

./docs-viewer/build/build_docs.py --scope <scope> --write
./docs-viewer/build/build_search.py --scope <scope> --write --only-doc-ids <ids> --remove-missing

This usually happens when source docs were added while the watcher was not running, leaving published payloads missing for unselected docs. The full docs payload pass catches the scope up, while the search step can remain targeted when affected ids are still safe.

When the changed-file count exceeds --targeted-search-threshold, or when the parsed snapshot cannot safely identify affected ids, both docs and search fall back to:

./docs-viewer/build/build_docs.py --scope <scope> --write
./docs-viewer/build/build_search.py --scope <scope> --write

That means a source change under one docs root keeps both:

  • the current scope docs-viewer JSON
  • the current scope docs-search artifact

in sync without touching the other docs scopes. For public scopes, these are the local published artifacts under docs-viewer/published/; the watcher does not publish to site/assets/data/.

For a configured Mermaid-source change, the watcher calls the same producer used by full and targeted Docs builds with only the changed same-basename SVG identity. It publishes and verifies that SVG directly; it does not run docs or search because ordinary diagram edits leave the Markdown token unchanged.

Change Types

The watcher treats these Markdown changes as rebuild triggers:

  • create
  • edit
  • delete
  • rename

It does this by comparing the current .md file set, file mtimes, and file sizes on each poll.

Configured Mermaid roots use the same poll/debounce state with a .mmd snapshot. Creating or editing a source renders only its same-basename SVG. Removing or renaming source does not automatically delete a previously published SVG; cleanup remains an explicit ownership decision. Parent-scope documents remain flat in their configured documents/ root. Each configured sub-scope has a separate watched documents/ root; hierarchy inside either corpus still comes from front matter rather than arbitrary source folders.

For targeted docs payloads and targeted search, it also keeps a parsed per-scope source snapshot from the last successful watcher rebuild. The snapshot maps filenames to front-matter-derived values including doc_id, title, parent_id, and viewable. The watcher does not assume filename equals doc_id.

Targeted affected-id rules:

  • added file: target the new doc_id
  • edited file: target the current doc_id
  • deleted file: target the previous doc_id
  • doc_id changed: target both old and new ids
  • title changed: target the doc plus direct children, because child search records include parent_title
  • parent_id changed: target only the changed doc under the current search schema
  • missing targeted docs prerequisites: run full same-scope docs payloads and keep targeted search when affected ids are known
  • parse failure, invalid docs tree, missing parsed snapshot, or threshold overflow: run full same-scope docs payloads and docs search

Watcher diagnostics are intentionally log-only. They report affected doc ids and fallback reasons, but avoid adding per-source-path detail beyond the existing changed-file detection line.

Operational Notes

  • bin/local-studio starts this watcher by default
  • bin/local-studio does not perform startup docs/docs-search rebuilds
  • scope create, rename, delete, and sub-scope config changes are reconciled without restarting the watcher
  • if a source root disappears before its matching config mutation is observed, the watcher waits without blocking other scopes; the subsequent config reconciliation either retires that state or replaces it with the new one
  • DOCS_WATCH_POLL_SECONDS, DOCS_WATCH_DEBOUNCE_SECONDS, and DOCS_WATCH_TARGETED_SEARCH_THRESHOLD default from .env.local for local runs, including when the watcher is started through bin/local-studio
  • manual rebuild commands remain available and are still the fallback path when you want explicit control
  • because watched source roots exclude published output roots, generated document, search, and SVG writes do not loop back into new watcher-triggered rebuilds
  • when the localhost Docs Viewer service writes a source doc and rebuilds the same scope itself, it leaves a short-lived suppression marker under var/docs/watch-suppressions/; the watcher uses that marker to avoid a redundant second rebuild for the same source change

Related References

  • Local Studio Runner
  • Docs Viewer Builder
  • Search Build Pipeline
